a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the required Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC).

We recommended and the management agreed to:

a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the OIC, General Services Officer to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all of its serviceable properties with the GSIS, and follow the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2018-002.

b) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in its proposed annual budget in the subsequent year for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS. In case of budget constraints, the MBO may charge such appropriation in the LDRRM Fund of the LGU.
We recommended that the Municipality observe the provisions of RA No. 9003 on the establishment and operationalization of its MRF and other solid waste management facilities.

We recommended that the MEEO shall prepare a 5-Year Business Plan (BP) for each existing LEE in order to determine whether each LEE is still operative and income-generating, or should instead be advised for service shedding, closure, or divestment. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare Financial Reports for each LEE, separate and distinct from the GF.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Office of the Municipal Treasurer to take immediate action in accounting the undeposited cash collections in prior years and deposit intact all the remaining cash collections reflected in the books;
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to make reconciliation on these negative balances and prepare correcting entries, if circumstances warrant; and
The management to impose stringent internal controls on its cash collection and timely deposit thereof to ensure compliance with the guidelines in the Manual and avoid or mitigate risks of defalcation, theft and losses.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
Unaccounted difference of the reported Cash in Bank – LCCA balances in the financial statements and its supporting schedules of the General Fund.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the difference between the balance of Cash in Bank accounts in the General Fund presented in the Financial Statements and its schedules.
Non-observance and incomplete timely submission of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on some bank accounts maintained by the LGU.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2023 on the abovementioned bank accounts in the Cash in Bank- LCCA account with no BRS and with attached Bank Statements; and
Ensure timely and complete submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts for all funds maintained by the LGU for CY 2024 and onwards.
Reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) remain uncorrected or unadjusted in the books of accounts.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the reconciling items in the books of accounts of the LGU; and
Submit to the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher taking up the adjustments.
Various stale checks were not cancelled.
The Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the concerned payees about the existence of a stale check and determine the validity of their claim;
The Municipal Treasurer to issue new checks as a replacement of stale checks if a valid claim exists due to the concerned payee;
The Municipal Accountant to provide adjusting entries to cancel the stale check; and
The Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the guidelines in the monitoring of outstanding checks.
Non-reconciliation on the bank account records of the LGU maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant and inadequate disclosure of LGU’s bank accounts in the Notes to Financial Statements.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records of the LGU’s bank accounts; and
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to completely disclose the list of bank accounts currently maintained by the LGU in all funds in the Notes to Financial Statements, beginning CY 2024 and onwards.
Existence of non-moving bank accounts and accounts maintained in unauthorized government depositary banks by the LGU.
The Office of the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough analysis on the balance of these non-moving depository bank accounts to ascertain whether the purpose/program where these bank accounts were opened have already been implemented/completed through retrieving any Memorandum of Agreements (MOA) entered into and laws and regulations governing these bank accounts;
Remit any unutilized balance of these depository bank accounts and/or revert the same to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, if warranted, and process the closure of those accounts as a result of thorough analysis; and
The Office of the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to secure a bank statement and immediately conduct a reconciliation on the balance of PNB bank account in the books, transfer any outstanding balance to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, and immediately process its closure.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the books to come up with a reliable balance of the RPT/SET Receivables and the corresponding Deferred RPT/SET;
The Municipal Treasurer exhausts all means to enforce in their locality the collection of RPT/SET against the taxpayers such as but not limited to sending demand letters and posting of the same in a regular manner highlighting the consequences of non-payment of taxes and the next possible action the taxing authority can do and/or conduct information and education campaign on RPT payment; and
The Municipal Treasurer to proactively forward the list of delinquent taxpayers to the Provincial Taxing authority and coordinate with them the imposition/implementation of the civil remedies in collecting taxes.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Market Supervisor with the supervision of the Municipal Treasurer, demand for the immediate payment of the delinquent accounts including surcharges. Otherwise, cause the immediate cancellation of the lease contracts with the stall owners and apply all other sanctions provided for in the Economic Enterprise Code and the Memorandum of Agreement/Lease Contract;
The Municipal Mayor enforce the strict implementation of the Revenue Code and Economic Enterprise Code; and
The Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Voucher to record the accrued income from market stall rentals and submit a copy to the Auditor.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our recommendation and that the management agreed to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough analysis on the reciprocal accounts and undertake the necessary reconciliation of the discrepancies noted;
Make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts as warranted; and
Prepare the corresponding disbursement voucher/s and pertinent documents to replenish the erroneous charging of fund.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the management direct the:
General Services Officer to regularly update the stock card by recording all issuances of supplies as reflected in the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS);
Office of the Municipal Accountant to maintain the required supplies ledger cards to facilitate the necessary reconciliation between the stock card; and
Inventory Committee to accomplish the required Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for all inventory items and submit the same to the auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
In addition, to require the:
MDRRMO, Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Treasurer to submit the distribution list/certificate of turnover and other necessary documents to facilitate the recording of expense for the distributed inventory items;
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries and provide the appropriate disclosures in the notes to financial statements pursuant to Paragraph 47 of IPSAS 12 including, but not limited to, the amount of inventory recognized as expenses during the year; and
Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the Perpetual Inventory Method by recording first the purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory accounts, and recognizing the corresponding expenses only upon issuances to the end-users.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct verification on other prepayments account to determine whether the purpose for which the amount was withheld was satisfied and coordinate with the appropriate office for the reconciliation of records; and
Record the appropriate adjusting and/or correcting entries to effect the results of verification.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
Direct the Inventory Committee and the newly designated GSO to prepare and conduct an annual physical inventory regularly at least once a year;
The General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant verify/investigate and clear all discrepancies of PPE balances between physical count and book records; and
The Municipal Accountant update the recording and balances of the PPE account once a copy furnished from the office of GSO and Inventory Committee is received.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the management require its responsible officers of the following:
Property Unit to physically segregate and promptly list down all obsolete and unserviceable PPE with complete data in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), duly signed by the Municipal Accountant, Inspector and Appraisal Committee, and approved by the Head of the Agency or authorized representative, and submit to the Disposal Committee for disposal as early as possible;
Disposal Committee to immediately dispose of all unserviceable/obsolete PPEs using the appropriate mode of disposal as determined, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from its sale, if warranted, and make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items; and
The GSO to submit the necessary documents together with the Official Receipt after disposal, as the basis to drop the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts to come up with the accurate balance of the PPE account in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386.
In the implementation of the foregoing, the provisions of the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties, be taken into consideration.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the following:
The Municipal GSO submit the Report on Local Road Network using the template provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and complete the necessary information in every field;
The Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, provide the cost segregation of every road network owned by the Municipality per road component for the prior years and submit the data to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO; and,
The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal GSO, disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the required disclosures on the Road Network System.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
Municipal Engineer submits the required Certificate of Completion of the completed projects and other billing documents to the Municipal Accountant for the adjustment/closing of the Construction in Progress accounts to its appropriate asset accounts;
Conduct a thorough analysis in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to update and correct the balances provided in their disclosures and reports and determine accordingly the status of completion of these projects if still part of the CIP accounts or not; and
Reclassify the completed project into its appropriate asset account, determine/compute the corresponding depreciation expenses and submit the corresponding journal entry voucher (JEV) to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough analysis on all recorded payables taking into consideration the recognition criteria and the validity of claims through completeness of supporting documents;
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to reduce the amount of payables if circumstances warrant; and
Make the necessary correction/s in the schedule to provide accurate information on the nature of the payables, so as not to mislead the users of the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to present the current portion of long term liabilities under the line-item Financial Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position for fair presentation of the account in the financial statements as prescribed in IPSAS No. 1.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to require the:
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the GSO and Engineering Office, to look into the details of these warranties so as to determine the ones which are due to be refunded to the suppliers/contractors; and
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the concerned creditors to process their claims for refunds, or require for a waiver in favor of the LGU to be accounted as the latter’s income.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our recommendation and the management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the collections to the National Treasury, or if not warranted, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to reclassify the balances to their appropriate accounts.
In addition, we recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify and record the collections and remittances of Withholding Tax, Res. Cert. Tax and 30% SEF Share to the Due to BIR account; and
Effect the proper liquidation of all implemented projects to the concerned agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds upon completion of the project, or seek authority from the issuing agency for the utilization of the excess funds.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Submit a reconciliation of surplus/(deficit) from operating activities with the net cash flow from operating activities in the Trust Fund for CY 2023;
Submit a disclosure on the breakdown of the adjustments indicated in Item d; and
Strictly observe the disclosure requirements in the presentation of the financial statements provided in IPSAS 22, onwards.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely preparation and submission of the financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations provided above, to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed of the following:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to ensure timely submission of the DVs, CRs and supporting documents to the Audit Team beginning CY 2024 onwards; and
Management institute effectively internal control mechanisms for the speedy processing of vouchers and documents in every transaction to cause its immediate recording in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to strictly observe the guidelines and procedures in the grant of cash advance for Confidential Funds as prescribed in the Joint Circular.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management direct the Municipal Health Officer to facilitate the processing of their reimbursement claims on the services they provided as an accredited health care provider, and utilize the funds to defray operating costs other than salaries, to maintain or upgrade equipment, plant or facility, and to maintain or improve the quality of service in the public sector in accordance with Section 34-A of the Revised IRR of the National Health Insurance Act of 2013.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
The LDRRMO to submit the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the COA office; and
The LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to ensure timely submission of the monthly Report on Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for CY 2024 and onwards.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed on each of the following observation:
Incomplete reversion of prior years’ PPAs in the LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2023 as well as non-update of the current year’s LDRRMF appropriations.
The LDRRMO to fully reprogram the funds recorded in the Special Trust Fund and include the reprogrammed PPAs in the revised LDRRMFIP; and
The LDRRMO, in the subsequent preparation of LDRRMFIP, to timely coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for any changes in the appropriations of each PPAs (reversion/supplemental/realignment) and effect such changes in the Investment Plan to ensure accurate monitoring in the utilization of funds and to avoid over/under utilization of funds in the implementation of its PPAs.
Non-implementation of some reprogrammed PPAs in the prior years’ fund totaling P1.466 million.
The LDRRMO to institute proper planning of the identified PPAs which includes providing doable timelines for its implementation and appropriating reasonable funds based on its desired output;
The LDRRMO to monitor and evaluate the full implementation of the PPAs and the optimal utilization of its funds by providing its status reports periodically so that any surplus of the funds after said PPAs have been fully implemented may be appropriated to another PPAs; and
The LDRRMO to evaluate the unimplemented PPAs in accordance with their attainability and consider the reprogramming of PPAs for the subsequent year that is still responsive to the needs of its constituents and the LGU with respect to disaster prevention, preparedness, response and recovery in the midst of the pandemic, and optimally implement identified PPAs within the prescribed timeline.
Non-reversion of unutilized LDRRM Fund in CY 2017 in the Special Trust Fund to the General Fund.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant, to revert the unutilized LDRRMF balances reflected in the Special Trust Fund to the General Fund after the 5-year withholding period; and
The Sangguniang Bayan, to identify other social services where the unutilized balances of the fund will be used for public benefit, through an enactment of an ordinance.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to provide correcting entries on the erroneous entries made and submit to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed that:
The HRMO to implement organizational structure, staffing pattern and analyze actual demand of workforce based on a per office service requirements, to come up with a most beneficial decision for the management and for its people;
The Budget Office in coordination with the HRMO and the management, determine the necessity of these plantilla positions and provide budget appropriations only to those found necessary; and
The HRMO, to proceed in filling up these identified necessary positions, to promote efficiency and welfare of their workforce.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed that:
The LGU of Tukuran to provide the written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General or the Government Corporate Counsel and the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit on the hiring of private lawyer and the reason to engage their services; and
Comply with the CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2015 in granting of overtime pay.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed of the following:
The LSB, in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budgets, require each public school principal to furnish the Board copies of their SIPs as basis for the preparation of the said budget to ensure effective collaboration between the LSB and the DepEd in addressing the needs of the public schools towards delivering quality education in the locality and to enable the audit team to conduct further verification as to the reasonableness of the PPAs formulated in the SEF Budget;
The LSB to request the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to furnish copies to the Board Actual SEF Income for the immediately preceding fiscal year, actual SEF Income for the first two quarters of the current year; and the estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and
The LSB to ensure timely approval of the SEF Budget.

Recommendations:

Maintain an additional FDP Board in a conspicuous place of the Municipality;
Update the posting of all the quarterly reports on the different FDP Boards maintained in the three (3) conspicuous places of the Municipality; and

2. Through the Office of the Accountant to transfer the unutilized LDRRM Funds of CY 2014 from “Trust Liability – DRRM” account in the Trust Fund books to the General Fund books to make it available for other social services as identified by the LGU.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary BRS to determine the nature and details of the difference between the balances of the accounting records and the bank statements and to prepare the appropriate adjusting entries to record any reconciling items in the books of accounts of the LGU.

Recommendation:

Recommendation:

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation of PPE accounts, and record the adjustment; and

Maintain index cards for each type of construction in progress account containing relevant information such as but not limited to name of project, location, funding source, total project cost, duration of the project, total cost incurred to date & progress billing for monitoring purposes.

The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts as warranted; and

We recommended and the management agreed thru the procuring unit to:
Submit an explanation in the procurement of testing kits above the price cap provided in the Circular imposed by the DOH.
Consistently monitor the price caps of the testing kits and other essential medical supplies imposed by the DOH by timely observing the guidelines posted by the said agency on their website and other platforms prior to its procurement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend management the following:
direct the Accounting Unit to conduct a thorough analysis on Cash in Bank – LCCA and facilitate the necessary reconciliation between the balances per book and per bank taking into consideration the adjusted balance as a result of the Bank Reconciliation Statement; and
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries if circumstances warrant.

We recommend management the following:
Set-up depreciation expense for the corresponding years where depreciation was not recognized and the corresponding JEV of the same to effect the adjustments in the ensuing year; and
Maintain PPELC of each PPE to record acquisition, description, custody, estimated useful life, depreciation, disposal and other information about the PPE.

We recommend management the following:
Create an inventory committee to conduct a physical count of the properties of the municipality;
Maintain a Property, Plant and Equipment Register to facilitate the conduct of year-end physical inventory, the municipality  use this as basis in the conduct and reconciliation of the accounts; and
Submit a copy of the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31st of each year as provided in Volume II of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) to facilitate verification of the inventory report.

We recommend management the following:
direct the Accounting Unit to conduct a thorough analysis on all recorded accounts payable taking into consideration the validity of claims through the existence of complete documents; and
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries if circumstances warrant.

We recommend management the following:
the Accounting Unit to make a representation to the GSIS on the unremitted premium covering the years from 1986 to 1997 for reconciliation;
If the obligations are indeed valid and exiting in the record of GSIS, cause the immediate remittance otherwise, secure a certification from GSIS that the same is not valid and existing; and
Record the necessary adjustment as it warrant.
We recommend that share in the proceeds of the Community Tax and Real Property Tax due to the barangay be released within the prescribed period provided for under R.A. 7160.

We recommend management the following:
Direct the Accounting Unit to conduct a thorough analysis on the reported balance of Other Payables taking into consideration the un-reconciled balance in the general fund and unsubstantiated adjustment in the trust fund; and
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries as circumstances warrant.

We recommend management to strictly adhere with the limitation on discretionary fund and extraordinary expenses as provided under Section 325 (h) of R.A. 7160 by ensuring that:
the amount of appropriation shall be equal to two percent (2%) of the actual receipts derived from basic real property tax in the next preceding year; and
the amount to be utilize shall be within the allocated budget or appropriation.

We recommend to management the following:
Only authorized deductions be allowed in order of preference; and
Ensure that an employee receives the required net take home pay mandated by law.

We recommend management the following:
Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to facilitate the review and evaluation process and attention is also invited to the penalty clause provision of said Circular; and
Submit copies of the contract and its integral documents enumerated in Appendix A for contract review.

The Local Development Council to monitor the implementation of the projects, programs and activities (PPAs) as incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan;

We recommend management to accomplish the various requirements that the LGU failed to comply with R.A. No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 as enumerated in the audit observation.

observations.recommendation:

observations.reason: The interval of the submissions is still inconsistent with the monthly requirement under COA Circular No. 2012.

observations.recommendation:
Management:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the COA at the beginning of the year, the list of the ongoing projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented. The list shall include the project name; brief description of the PPA; contractor or supplier, mode of procurement; funding source; cost or approved budget; project duration including start and completion dates; and location;

Notify the public through signboards of their projects location and inform the SA thru ATL that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  should  comply  with
pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 on the  preparation  of  BRS
to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account.

We recommend that Management requires the  Municipal  Accountant  and
the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the  account  in  their
respective books of accounts, to verify any  unreconciled  balances  in  the
Cash  in  Bank  accounts  for  all  funds  and  to  prepare  the   necessary
adjustments to reconcile the balances per Cashbooks and SLs.

We recommend all Accountable Officers of the Municipality  of  Tukuran
be  required  to  prepare  their  respective  RAAF  and  the   OIC-Municipal
Treasurer to consolidate the RAAF and submit the  CRAAF  together  with  all
RAAFs at the end of each month to  the  Audit  Team  through  the  Municipal
Accounting Office.

It is recommended that the Municipal  Accounting  Office  maintain  an
updated subsidiary ledger of each collecting officers upon  receipt  of  the
daily RCD for easy validation  on  the  posting  of  daily  collections  and
request  additional  personnel  to  perform  such   task   (maintenance   of
subsidiary ledger for individual collector).

It is recommended  that  the  personnel-in-charge  shall  monitor  the
renewal of the fidelity bond, the proper bonding and designation of all  the
accountable  officers  including  casual  employees  in   the   municipality
pursuant to Section  182  of  Government  Accounting  and  Auditing  Manual,
Volume I and other pertinent rules and regulations to  safeguard  government
funds and property and to recover from the insurance any amount in  case  of
losses of funds thru misuse, thief/robbery.

The Municipal Accountant should comply with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 on the preparation of BRS.

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer likewise reconcile the balances of the account in their respective books of accounts and to verify any unreconciled balances in the Cash in Bank accounts for all funds and to prepare the necessary adjustments to reconcile the balances per Cashbooks and SLs.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR | The Local Chief Executive (LCE) has already issued a memorandum order to the responsible persons to continue with the monthly reconciliation and effect the adjustments.
observations.reason:
Management has still not able to submit on time or has failed to submit the BRS within the time prescribed. 

Reiterated in the 2016 AAR.
